parsedContent.feedback: The answer shows no understanding of the question. A spider is not considered a true insect because it belongs to a different class (Arachnida) and has a different body structure, not just because of the number of legs. True insects belong to the class Insecta.
parsedContent.correctedAnswer: A spider is not considered a true insect because it belongs to a different class (Arachnida) and has a different body structure, not just because of the number of legs. True insects belong to the class Insecta.
